calendar.matters:
DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Mar-03-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances, Not contested, The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

The Application of Plaintiffs Chelsey Taylor, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Gary Oldford, Deceased; and William Callow, Billie Jean Oldford and Lillian Oldford, as Wrongful Death Heirs of Gary Oldford, Deceased, for Entry of Default Judgment against Defendant Rich-Tex, Inc., in the amount of $2,044,155.03 is GRANTED.

The Court has reviewed the prove-up packet including the Judicial Council Form CIV-100, the memorandum, Mr. Nevin's declaration and the attached exhibits, which are admitted. The Court finds Defendant has been served with process, failed to answer or appear and defend the action within the time allowed by law, and default has been duly entered. As such, Plaintiffs are entitled to the relief sought in the Application. The Court intends to execute the proposed judgment accompanying the application.

Judgment signed by the court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Ed Santos and Jackie Alameda; Matter not reported; Bailiff: Not Present. (D304/EPS)
